mnemonics: CLT Stands for 'Can't Lose Truth': The Central Limit Theorem guarantees that the sampling distribution of the mean will be approximately normal for a large enough sample size, no matter the population's shape.

Sampling Distribution is a 'Statistic's Statistic': It's the distribution of a statistic (like the mean) calculated from all possible samples of a specific size, describing the behavior of the statistic itself.
keyPoints: A sampling distribution describes the probability distribution of a sample statistic (e.g., the mean, proportion) obtained from a large number of samples drawn from a specific population.

The Central Limit Theorem (CLT) states that the sampling distribution of the sample mean will be approximately normally distributed for a sufficiently large sample size (n ≥ 30 is a common rule of thumb), regardless of the population's distribution.

The standard deviation of the sampling distribution of the mean is called the standard error (SE), and it is calculated as SE = σ/√n, where σ is the population standard deviation and n is the sample size.
commonMistakes: Confusing the sampling distribution (distribution of a statistic) with the sample distribution (distribution of the data in a single sample) or the population distribution (distribution of the entire population).
Misapplying the Central Limit Theorem for small sample sizes (n < 30) or for statistics other than the mean (e.g., the median) without verifying the conditions.
